title: myrmecodia albertisii subsp. albertisii
intro: Myrmecodia albertisii subsp. albertisii, also known as the ant plant, is a type of epiphytic plant that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This plant is notable for its unique relationship with ants, providing them with shelter and food in exchange for nutrients and protection from herbivores. The medicinal properties of Myrmecodia albertisii subsp. albertisii include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that are said to have health benefits including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
